subject: Trump announces national emergency for COVID-19
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kathaleen Wall for Congress
body:
It is during sobering times in history like today that you and I are reminded of the strength and resilience of Americans. | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/3aafd5786e99/trump-announces-national-emergency-for-covid-19?e=01c99b7fbd)
---
| |
---
| | Hi friend,

It is during sobering times in history like today that you and I are reminded of the strength and resilience of Americans.

President Trump announced hours ago a state of national emergency in response to the pandemic of the COVID-19 virus (also known as the "coronavirus").

Now is not the time for partisan politics, but for you and I to stand together with every American to follow the President’s lead.

**Here is what you need to know:**

• The President’s order will allow hospitals to waive existing laws and requirements to expedite the process to help those needing testing and treatment.

• The order will also allow hospitals to bring in additional medical personnel immediately if there is an increase in cases in that community by eliminating the approval period.

• This order also extends vital financial assistance, up to $50 billion to be exact, to help keep communities, just like ours, safe from the COVID-19 virus outbreak.

• Old telehealth laws will be waived so this modern approach to healthcare can be fully utilized during this outbreak.

The President’s leadership has been at its high point, bringing together business competitors to put aside any rivalries so all of them can address urgent medical testing and equipment needs and provide the necessary testing equipment and processes to address this serious virus.

The President has brought together federal and local government entities to create a unique public-private partnership and put the needs of Americans first.

The massive mobilization of public and private resources will help America overcome this pandemic.

_“Our most effective weapon is to limit the damage to our country, to our people, and slow the spread of the virus itself. The choices we make and the precautions we put into place are critical to overcoming the virus, reducing the duration of the pandemic,”_ \- President Trump.

Visit [**KathaleenWall.com/covid19**](https://KathaleenWall.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6732b2a961d1a34b05e3194bb&id=c6d4411b70&e=01c99b7fbd) for a complete list of local, state, and federal healthcare resources. Whether you live in Fort Bend, Brazoria, or Harris Counties, we have compiled the numbers to call for any questions you may have.

Let’s check on our neighbors, especially those who are most at risk, seniors, or those with chronic health conditions. Let’s stay smart and safe. Let’s conquer this virus together.

God bless you,
Kathaleen Wall
---

name: Dean Browning
subject: ICYMI: Buy and Lie Lisa Scheller
body:
---
| | | | | |
---
| Dear Patriot,
While the bid for the Democratic Presidential nominee is now between a self-proclaimed Communist and an addled long-time Democratic swamp creature, my campaign is still focused on one thing: defeating Representative Susan Wild in November. But before I can take on Susan Wild, I must secure the Republican nomination on April 28th. Let me tell you, I am up against the Establishment and their preferred candidate who is Pro-Sanctuary City, Pro-Abortion and who says one thing while doing another. I want you to know, , that Lisa Scheller is our local version of Michael Bloomberg.
Just like Michael Bloomberg, Lisa Scheller wants to "Buy and Lie" her way into office by using her money to flood your mailbox and cable news to try and re-write her past. For example:
* Lisa says she wants to work with President Trump, yet she was a Never Trumper and gave the maximum donation to John Kasich when he ran against the President. Lisa did not mention President Trump once during her campaign kickoff and when asked, she said she did not want to "make this a litmus test on Trump".
* Lisa says she wants to eliminate Sanctuary Cities, yet as Commissioner she voted for the resolution that made Lehigh County a Sanctuary County.
* Lisa says she is pro-life, yet she has said abortion should be legal and she refuses to make public her answers to the National Right to Life's Congressional Candidate Questionnaire.
* Lisa says she is for term limits, yet as Commissioner she sponsored and approved the ordinance that prevented Lehigh County voters from being able to decide on a ballot initiative that would have set term limits on Lehigh County politicians.
* Lisa says she opposes corporate handouts, yet the huge corporation she owns took our tax dollars - stimulus money from President Obama - to line her own pockets.

Actions speak louder than words and I need your financial support to take down "Buy and Lie" Lisa Scheller who is being backed by Washington special interests who don't care about you and me. Will you help me prove that the only way to run a campaign is with the support of hard-working Americans, like yourself, by donating $100, $50, or even just $25 today?

| I firmly believe Lisa Scheller's current views are a matter of convenience to try and get her through the primary. Her past actions prove that she is 180 degrees from my views on ending illegal immigration, on eliminating sanctuary cities, on protecting the life of the unborn from the moment of conception and on draining the Swamp. But , my biggest fear is that if she's elected, she will be just another "RINO" and fail to back President Trump as he fights to Keep America Great. From looking at her previous voting record... this is a real threat-- she is a wolf in sheep's clothes. We cannot let this happen!! The people of Lehigh Valley deserve someone in Congress who is unwavering in their conservative values and has not sold out to Washington special interests.
Most importantly, the Lehigh Valley deserves a candidate who will be an ally of President Donald J. Trump and who will support his agenda unequivocally! I urge you to donate to my campaign today so that I can defeat my "Republican" primary opponent in April. A donation of $100, $50, or even just $25 will help me support our President and bring conservative values back to our district.

name: Dr. Chuck Dietzen
subject: Weekly Roundup

body:
Dr. Chuck's week in review
[ ](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/8358a878bca5394edf60ac364a9f256a/33363/9b8074f01320eaaf/affea29b6514bae8c3d6b838967d376c/33363) Date: March 13, 2020 **DR. CHUCK FOR CONGRESS** | |
---
[ | |
---|---|--- ](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/8358a878bca5394edf60ac364a9f256a/33363/9b8074f01320eaaf/967b44a12a68bb4cc3d6b838967d376c/33363)
|
---
**FROM THE TRAIL**
**MARION COFFEE WITH THE CANDIDATE**
It was a great morning in Marion, Indiana, for our Coffee with the Candidate at the 9th Street Cafe, followed by a stop at Bella Villa Hair & Nail Salon. My team and I enjoyed seeing old friends, meeting new ones, and sharing my vision as to why I want to be your next Congressman here in Indiana’s 5th District.
|
---
One Zone Chamber Event - Carmel
This past Wednesday, I attended the One Zone Chamber event in Carmel. I enjoyed meeting all of the business owners and taking some time to talk with folks from the Hamilton County Youth Assistance Program and learning of the advocacy for youth ages 3-17 who are facing challenging life circumstances.
Meeting with Westfield Mayor Andy Cook
I enjoyed my conversation with Westfield Mayor Andy Cook. Westfield is a thriving city on the move. His commitment to the families and especially the children in this community is truly inspiring.
|
---
**LUNCH WITH HARTFORD CITY MAYOR DAN ECKSTEIN**
It was good to meet the new Mayor of Hartford City, Dan Eckstein. He has a big vision and after meeting with him, I was impressed with his agenda.
|
---
**FISHERS GOP MEET THE CANDIDATES NIGHT**
On Thursday my team and I attended the Fishers GOP Club meeting, where we heard from some of our local candidates. Hamilton County truly has some quality leaders and candidates.

|
---
**CATCH UP WITH DR. CHUCK**
**TIPTON COUNTY GOP DINNER**
Saturday, March 14th
6pm – 8pm
Tipton County Fairgrounds
1200 S. Main St
Tipton, Indiana
**COFFEE WITH THE CANDIDATE**
Thursday, March 19th
8am – 9am
Gingers Cafe
1111 South 10th St
Noblesville, Indiana
|

Dear Friend, "A widespread failure in the United States to __invest__ in public health has left local and state health departments struggling to respond..." That's from an article in the New York Times. The emphasis on "invest" is mine. It echoes my core message for the last two and a half years. Congress is more focused on winning the next election than it is in investing in the future of America. Members collectively satisfy the needs of those most likely to help them get reelected, and our long-term investments in health care, education, and infrastructure suffer. When asked recently about budget cuts to the CDC, the NIH, and the WHO, and whether those cuts now gave him pause, the President responded in part - "Some people we cut, they haven't been used for many, many years. ... And rather than spending the money - and I'm a business person - I don't like having thousands of people around when you don't need them." Government is not business. Government requires investments in preparedness to protect the lives and liberty not only of today's citizens, but of generations to come. These investments can't be measured by returns on a balance sheet. One of our kids is coming home from college. Recently, she had a high fever and still has a lingering cough. Was it a mild case of COVID-19? Should she be tested? Should she be isolated? Are our other children at risk? We don't know, and we can't find out, because as a nation we cut our investment, and we aren't prepared. body:
_****_
One of the Center for Disease Control's (CDC) key recommendations to curb the spread of coronavirus (COVID-19) is for sick people to stay home. The United States, however, does not currently have National Paid Sick leave.

That means for many workers, staying home sick would result in a direct loss of income. For folks living paycheck to paycheck, losing an income is not an option.

**That 's exactly why Linda is urging Congress to pass National Paid Sick Leave. [Will you support the roughly 57 million American workers without paid sick leave by signing on?](https://act.myngp.com/el/dLF8_Sd2aCaUXICDozsjcFCTEWsUav9VXedFsmb6pKw=/sfkSILCwQHh3r1xU2uLA0IEZ_kgr47C4e5fXTcSzz7E=)**
[_****_](https://act.myngp.com/el/dLF8_Sd2aCaUXICDozsjcFCTEWsUav9VXedFsmb6pKw=/sfkSILCwQHh3r1xU2uLA0IEZ_kgr47C4e5fXTcSzz7E=)
The United States is the _only_ developed nation without a national policy on paid sick leave. This is shameful -- _especially_ during a global health emergency. Not only is it unfair to the folks without paid sick leave, but it also puts the rest of our communities at higher risk of getting sick.

name: Nicole Malliotakis
subject: Coronavirus Update
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY NICOLE FOR NEW YORK (FEDERAL)
body:
Friends- Yesterday President Trump declared a national state of emergency to mobilize federal resources to meet the challenges we face with the recent coronavirus outbreak. This important step is the latest in a series or actions we are taking to make sure we protect the health and safety of Americans.
| | | | [](https://nicolemalliotakis.acemlnb.com/lt.php?s=e671297fc19753f4b1f2ae7718aefd90&i=119A160A1A1978)
---
| | |
---
| | Friends-

Yesterday President Trump declared a national state of emergency to mobilize federal resources to meet the challenges we face with the recent coronavirus outbreak. This important step is the latest in a series or actions we are taking to make sure we protect the health and safety of Americans.

At the beginning of the month, I joined my colleagues in the state legislature to pass an emergency package helping municipalities and hospitals with the cost of healthcare personnel, protective equipment for first responders, testing for the virus, sanitization of transit systems and airports, and other critical public safety steps.

Since then, I wrote to the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services joining Governor Cuomo and others in urging FDA approval of New York’s request to contract with private labs to increase testing capacity and decrease turn around time. The new labs will be on line this upcoming week.

Yesterday, all Staten Island elected officials met at Borough Hall and subsequently called on Mayor de Blasio to close public schools and senior centers.

In the meantime, I’ve been in contact with the senior centers serving my district and delivered hand sanitizers to those which were lacking.

If you or someone you know is a senior citizen who lives alone and cannot cook or shop, Meals on Wheels is still accepting new intakes for home meal delivery. On Staten Island, please call 718-727-4435 and in South Brooklyn, please call 718-680-3530.

I’ve also been in contact with the NYPD and City Hall to ensure our police officers on the street have proper protective equipment. The Mayor announced 250,000 additional face masks will be allocated and increased based on need.

All area hospitals ask that you call your primary physician first if you have the symptoms associated with coronavirus (fever, cough, difficulty breathing) to get proper instructions on next steps.

I am utilizing social media to provide regular updates on confirmed cases and school closures as received.

You can follow me on [Facebook](https://nicolemalliotakis.acemlnb.com/lt.php?s=e671297fc19753f4b1f2ae7718aefd90&i=119A160A1A1979) and [Twitter](https://nicolemalliotakis.acemlnb.com/lt.php?s=e671297fc19753f4b1f2ae7718aefd90&i=119A160A1A1980).

clean_body: Friends- Yesterday President Trump declared a national state of emergency to mobilize federal resources to meet the challenges we face with the recent coronavirus outbreak. This important step is the latest in a series or actions we are taking to make sure we protect the health and safety of Americans. | | | | | | | | | Friends- Yesterday President Trump declared a national state of emergency to mobilize federal resources to meet the challenges we face with the recent coronavirus outbreak. This important step is the latest in a series or actions we are taking to make sure we protect the health and safety of Americans. At the beginning of the month, I joined my colleagues in the state legislature to pass an emergency package helping municipalities and hospitals with the cost of healthcare personnel, protective equipment for first responders, testing for the virus, sanitization of transit systems and airports, and other critical public safety steps. Since then, I wrote to the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services joining Governor Cuomo and others in urging FDA approval of New York’s request to contract with private labs to increase testing capacity and decrease turn around time. The new labs will be on line this upcoming week. Yesterday, all Staten Island elected officials met at Borough Hall and subsequently called on Mayor de Blasio to close public schools and senior centers. In the meantime, I’ve been in contact with the senior centers serving my district and delivered hand sanitizers to those which were lacking. If you or someone you know is a senior citizen who lives alone and cannot cook or shop, Meals on Wheels is still accepting new intakes for home meal delivery. On Staten Island, please call 718-727-4435 and in South Brooklyn, please call 718-680-3530. I’ve also been in contact with the NYPD and City Hall to ensure our police officers on the street have proper protective equipment. The Mayor announced 250,000 additional face masks will be allocated and increased based on need. All area hospitals ask that you call your primary physician first if you have the symptoms associated with coronavirus (fever, cough, difficulty breathing) to get proper instructions on next steps. I am utilizing social media to provide regular updates on confirmed cases and school closures as received. Peter,

A year ago today, Governor Kim Reynolds signed an emergency disaster proclamation as a result of the horrific flooding across our state.

With record levels of water due to heavy snow and rainfall, warmer temperatures, frozen soil, and over-saturated grounds, significant damage was caused across our state. Crops were ruined, soil weakened for future growing seasons, **resulting in $1.6 billion of damage.**

Despite all of these serious impacts, little action has been taken to change course and protect us from future occurrences like this. We need to be taking steps in Congress to address the climate crisis right in front of us.

After the floods, homes were significantly damaged, and schools, military bases, and farms in the Midwest had to scale back operations.

We need to make sure we’re prepared -- because the truth is, flooding is not going to stop given our current climate crisis. More and more scientists are warning us about continued heavy rainfall, rising temperatures, and more drastic swings in weather.

And worst of all -- what we don’t fix now will make our children’s future bleak.

I am where I am today because my parents and grandparents left me with opportunities. We must do the same for our children. **I have always believed that we aren’t inheriting the land from our parents, we’re borrowing it from our children.**

subject: A few thoughts on President Trump and COVID-19
body:
| | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/245b1df0e874/calling-all-patriots-12245746?e=795c6d9796)
---
| |
---
| | Peter, On Friday President Trump declared a national emergency regarding the COVID-19 virus. On its surface, the proclamation was a procedural move that set into motion a series of sweeping support and restorative measures that will go to the heart of what is needed to fight this virus. His words were reassuring and at the same time impressive in the scope of what was being brought to bear for the crisis.

There was also a stunning development in the manner in which services would be provided. The President waved large swaths of regulations and red tape that otherwise would hamper the allocation of beds in hospitals and the distribution of critical equipment. What is unique and bold about his plan is that the private sector is partnering with the federal government in providing a dramatically expanded number of tests for the public. Pharmaceutical companies will provide these tests and testing sites will be established in retailers such as Walmart, Target and CVS. As a result, scientists and epidemiologists will have more information and will have it much faster regarding the scope of the virus and the spread of the virus and will then be able to treat it in an effective targeted fashion. It is not guaranteed but it is possible that this pandemic will level off quicker with less loss of life and danger to the public.

In essence, the remarkable ability of our free market system is now partnering with the federal government in delivering better health services to the public and is doing it faster and more efficiently. In the end, I also believe we will find that it was done at lower costs. Gone are the days when in a crisis we all waited nervously to see what the government will do. I am reminded of Ronald Reagan’s admonition that the worse thing we could hear is “I am from the government and I am here to help”. President Regan’s words ring true and now, President Trump has shown us what is possible. In times of crisis, Americans have always risen to the challenge. It is reassuring to know that our free-market capitalist system has come to the rescue to help resolve a crisis that only a day ago rocked the markets and our nerves. Instead of a big government response, free market capitalism and American know-how are solving the problem. As a result, conservative’s vision of a health care system based on a free market solution just got a shot in the arm. Thank you President Trump!

Best,
\--Janet Barresi

body:
---
| | | | | | [ ](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0016SkxwlLL2K9V01Sl80-TU5GPbSyEHjLxhEmU3CVWPz9fTQ0EU59T8rqxwhlji4ZOYjIvN0-x9RTTglHesTJYSuuRo3IFgiBei__Tsg8G73WLj1wNLA5YLQFc4zfN-BjKsIxRTjbHLYMITDGa6wQi17tm_eNBxJbl9F-op292rHYQJDCZWK6ZeQ==&c=9pEemWHlGq88xlXRLlR4GlK442xadH7zc9hOMdqGqsZBIxtdQz5llw==&ch=UA7A1YbWj_jUZQaBzPHNMXkX24cRqaOC3QWAPQHerOFW1E_PLlSSXQ==)
---
| Peter,
In uncertain times, cool heads must prevail. Our community is doing everything right to keep our families and each other safe. Many of us, however, have become aware of some unsettling news regarding cybercriminals exploiting the coronavirus situation.
We don't yet know the extent of their email phishing scams, but I want to share this information as a PSA to our community, so that you can avoid these criminals and stay out of harm's way. Please see the CNN article below and keep yourselves safe and well this weekend.
> Cybercriminals are trying to cash in on the coronavirus, experts warn
> By Donie O'Sullivan, CNN Business
> Thu March 12, 2020
> Hackers are sending emails purporting to provide information about the coronavirus but are designed to trick people into handing over their passwords, experts at the leading cyber threat intelligence firm FireEye said Thursday. Some even contain malware that could take over a victim's computer.
> "We've seen financially motivated actors using coronavirus themed phishing in many campaigns, with dramatic month-over-month volume increases from January through to today," FireEye said in a brief prepared for media and shared with CNN Business.
> Ben Read, senior manager, cyber espionage analysis at FireEye, explained that hackers regularly exploit crises and other news events that people might be concerned about in attacks. "It's the same tactic with a different coat of paint," he said.
> But rarely does an event garner so much shared global interest and concern as the coronavirus, he said. As a result hackers have particularly focused on the pandemic. Email has been an important mode of communication, with service providers, government offices and schools using it to share information about safety measures, closures and other changes. Hackers, in turn, may try to mimic those messages, Read said.
> FireEye provided details of what it said was a malicious email that had the subject line "D-19 Everything you need to know."
> The email contained a malicious link to a coronavirus FAQ document.
> He advised people to take the same standard steps they should always take like exercising caution before clicking on links in emails and to be wary of any emails or messages that you aren't expecting.
> "If you get an email that looks like it is from the WHO (World Health Organization) and you don't normally get emails from the WHO, you should be cautious," Read said.
---
| Please stay alert and be on the lookout for these email scams. I also encourage you to forward this information to your friends!
---
|
---
| Geneveive Collins Candidate for Congressional District 32

clean_body: | | | | | | == | Peter, In uncertain times, cool heads must prevail. Our community is doing everything right to keep our families and each other safe. Many of us, however, have become aware of some unsettling news regarding cybercriminals exploiting the coronavirus situation. We don't yet know the extent of their email phishing scams, but I want to share this information as a PSA to our community, so that you can avoid these criminals and stay out of harm's way. Please see the CNN article below and keep yourselves safe and well this weekend. Cybercriminals are trying to cash in on the coronavirus, experts warn By Donie O'Sullivan, CNN Business Thu March 12, 2020 Hackers are sending emails purporting to provide information about the coronavirus but are designed to trick people into handing over their passwords, experts at the leading cyber threat intelligence firm FireEye said Thursday. Some even contain malware that could take over a victim's computer. "We've seen financially motivated actors using coronavirus themed phishing in many campaigns, with dramatic month-over-month volume increases from January through to today," FireEye said in a brief prepared for media and shared with CNN Business. Ben Read, senior manager, cyber espionage analysis at FireEye, explained that hackers regularly exploit crises and other news events that people might be concerned about in attacks. "It's the same tactic with a different coat of paint," he said. But rarely does an event garner so much shared global interest and concern as the coronavirus, he said. As a result hackers have particularly focused on the pandemic. Email has been an important mode of communication, with service providers, government offices and schools using it to share information about safety measures, closures and other changes. Hackers, in turn, may try to mimic those messages, Read said. FireEye provided details of what it said was a malicious email that had the subject line "D-19 Everything you need to know." The email contained a malicious link to a coronavirus FAQ document. He advised people to take the same standard steps they should always take like exercising caution before clicking on links in emails and to be wary of any emails or messages that you aren't expecting. "If you get an email that looks like it is from the WHO (World Health Organization) and you don't normally get emails from the WHO, you should be cautious," Read said. | Please stay alert and be on the lookout for these email scams. I also encourage you to forward this information to your friends! | | Geneveive Collins Candidate for Congressional District 32 | == | == == == | | | == | | | Paid for by Collins for Texas, Inc. | | Collins for Texas, Inc. | 6119A Greenville Ave., Ste 423, Dallas, TX 75206 | | Sent by mailto:gdc@gcforcongress.com

name: Colin Lauderdale, Campaign Manager
subject: Campaign update re: coronavirus
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Booker for Kentucky
body:
I’m reaching out to you for the first time today because I wanted to personally update you on what the campaign is doing to address the COVID-19 pandemic.
#
Nice to meet you, I’m Colin. I’m the campaign manager for Charles Booker.
I’m reaching out to you for the first time today because I wanted to personally update you on what the campaign is doing to address the COVID-19 pandemic.
Right now, we’re figuring out new ways to keep moving the campaign forward and keep fighting for Charles’ message while putting your health & safety first. body:
This last week made it clear. The COVID-19 outbreak is a national healthcare crisis | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/310a22aa3dc9/this-crisis?e=140a9b81e7)
---
| |
---
| |
#
This week, I traveled to Washington DC and Chicago. The campaign for the people of IL-16 is ramping up and, when I left home Monday, COVID-19 was on the periphery of our collective consciousness.

By Tuesday evening, the streets of Washington DC largely deserted and it was clear that**we are facing a real public health crisis, and we are facing it _now_.**

I’m so grateful for leadership like that of **Rep. Katie Porter** and **my friend Rep. Lauren Underwood** , whose leadership is helping to push the CDC to provide no-cost testing to the general public and to take this threat very seriously. **When I join them in Congress next year, this is exactly the kind of battle I’ll be there to fight.** How we can keep one another safe and healthy.

**But we need more — paid sick leave, moratoriums on evictions, and funding for the CDC so we can be better prepared for the inevitable next time.**

We’ve taken steps to help keep IL-16 safe — canceled all upcoming public events, switched from canvasses to phone banks — but that means we need your help now more than ever.

[Can you pitch in $20 or $30 to help us keep the momentum of this campaign going?](https://daniforillinois.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=16d8d19eb3a417b5b09061c0d&id=6ade52e7f5&e=140a9b81e7)

Stay safe.

Dani Brzozowski

name: Christy Smith
subject: Tell Mitch McConnell: Call the Senate back and pass a coronavirus bill
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Christy Smith for Congress
body:
| []()
---

| | Peter,

With the coronavirus continuing to spread, now is not the time for politics.

The House did their job last night and passed a bill to put families first by offering paid sick leave, job protection, and protecting access to nutrition and health care programs. **But Mitch McConnell is refusing to bring the Senate back from recess and pass this urgently needed bill.**

Enough is enough. We need to raise our voices loudly and force these politicians to listen to the people -- take this crisis seriously and do everything they can to get it under control.

**[Please join me and our partners in calling on the Senate to pass legislation that will put families first and keep Americans safe.](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6b81b/29776869/af64409/569fa8ae/3586563329/VEsH/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDAzMTRfY292aWRNRy1mdWxsIn0%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "http://www.paction.us/coronavirus-legislation/christy-smith-2?source=em20_200314_covidMG-full")** [ADD YOUR NAME](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6b81b/29776869/af64409/569fa8ae/3586563329/VEsE/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDAzMTRfY292aWRNRy1mdWxsIn0%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "http://www.paction.us/coronavirus-legislation/christy-smith-2?source=em20_200314_covidMG-full") My mother was a nurse during the AIDS crisis, and she tended to HIV-positive patients. That was a crisis made worse by our elected leaders' refusal to acknowledge how serious and widespread it really was. And that choice cost people their lives.

Americans deserve the truth and swift action from our leaders right now.

But unfortunately, we're going to have to demand it from certain politicians in Washington.

**[Add your name to call on the Senate to pass the House's bill that takes strong action to curb the coronavirus crisis.](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6b81b/29776869/af64409/569fa8ae/3586563329/VEsF/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDAzMTRfY292aWRNRy1mdWxsIn0%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "http://www.paction.us/coronavirus-legislation/christy-smith-2?source=em20_200314_covidMG-full")** [ADD YOUR NAME](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6b81b/29776869/af64409/569fa8ae/3586563329/VEsC/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDAzMTRfY292aWRNRy1mdWxsIn0%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "http://www.paction.us/coronavirus-legislation/christy-smith-2?source=em20_200314_covidMG-full") Thank you. And stay safe and healthy,

Christy

P.S. Accurate information is critical during these times. Check out the California Department of Public Health's website for any information you might need: [https://www.cdph.ca.gov/Programs/CID/DCDC/Pages/Immunization/nCOV2019.aspx](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6b81b/29776869/af64409/569fa8af/3586563329/VEsD/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://www.cdph.ca.gov/Programs/CID/DCDC/Pages/Immunization/nCOV2019.aspx") |

name: Jason Yates
subject: Sunday Live Streaming Event: America, it’s time to pray
body:
| | Peter, Our President has declared tomorrow, Sunday, March 15, to be a National Day of Prayer. Here’s what President Trump tweeted: _We are a country that, throughout our history, has looked to God for protection and strength in times like these. No matter where you may be, I encourage you to turn towards prayer in an act of faith. Together, we will easily PREVAIL!_ **America, it’s time to pray.** [](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66iVl2bNHZpIGlNoQAduC0VVdQwUylQtNErWARJjql45DSbE7VGdSK7yyaoRxkIi-PiA&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e200314com01&n=1) I want to invite you to pray with us tomorrow. Join us LIVE at 10:30 am CT for an online service we are calling “America, It’s Time to Pray.” This streaming prayer service from World Outreach Church with Pastor Allen Jackson will provide an inspiring time of worship and teaching, along with an interview with Pastor Rob Morgan, author of _100 Verses that Changed America_ , discussing Biblical lessons from critical moments in our nation’s history. Please take a moment and watch this special invitation prepared for you by Pastor Jackson. [](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66iVl2bNHZpIGlNoQAduC0VVdQwUylQtNErWARJjql45DSbE7VGdSK7yyaoRxkIi-PiA&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e200314com01&n=2) I hope you will be able to join us. You can watch the National Day of Prayer service on Sunday at 10:30 am CT from our [Facebook page](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=27Ax0UmWOX2J8J_QwK9Z6sQ5cX_bEu8cPkWYJ2Mr5-wZKXu3dY5XWtaPM4h3adih&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e200314com01&n=3) or [YouTube channel](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=jalJvum2JQxpPvfjGmN5k-T8lZnUpXGIBq_HKeZtmWw&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e200314com01&n=4). Faithfully, Jason Yates
CEO
[](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=FvZODUdzRCV4_QVEoUI5Y_LYDGbWPUdf00keax2EMv8&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e200314com01&n=5)
